creator: john@saltant.com
text:
(In reply to Gerald Pfeifer from comment #2)

> Is there anything on your system that is different from default?

Yes. I have a non-empty /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/{,$SET-}make.conf. I am also carrying a number of patches in the ports tree, mostly associated with submitted bugs awaiting commit to Python-related ports. Beyond that, I'm not sure which deviations from a clean install might be relevant in this case.

> Any processor-specific settings?  (/etc/make.conf or otherwise)

None that are obvious to me.

From /var/run/dmesg.boot:

====
CPU: Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2630 0 @ 2.30GHz (2300.05-MHz K8-class CPU)
  Origin="GenuineIntel"  Id=0x206d7  Family=0x6  Model=0x2d  Stepping=7
  Features=0xbfebfbff<FPU,VME,DE,PSE,TSC,MSR,PAE,MCE,CX8,APIC,SEP,MTRR,PGE,MCA,CMOV,PAT,PSE36,CLFLUSH,DTS,ACPI,MMX,FXSR,SSE,SSE2,SS,HTT,TM,PBE>
  Features2=0x1fbee3ff<SSE3,PCLMULQDQ,DTES64,MON,DS_CPL,VMX,SMX,EST,TM2,SSSE3,CX16,xTPR,PDCM,PCID,DCA,SSE4.1,SSE4.2,x2APIC,POPCNT,TSCDLT,AESNI,XSAVE,OSXSAVE,AVX>
  AMD Features=0x2c100800<SYSCALL,NX,Page1GB,RDTSCP,LM>
  AMD Features2=0x1<LAHF>
  XSAVE Features=0x1<XSAVEOPT>
  VT-x: PAT,HLT,MTF,PAUSE,EPT,UG,VPID
  TSC: P-state invariant, performance statistics
====

> Can you reproduce this on different machines, or only one system
(to rule out hardware issues)?

I will have to study the logs more closely to answer this with confidence. Nominally I run two poudriere build machines, and the other (more modern hardware with a substantially similar configuration) builds successfully. However, prior to upgrading to 12.1, I launched a 12.1 EC2 instance to generate a package repo, but I did not iterate until reaching 100% success before rsync-ing the results and proceeding with the upgrade.

PS: Sorry about the dupe. Bugzilla appeared to fail on my first attempt.
